creativity_vs_memorization.py.value:
"""
Two-stage creativity-vs-memorization evaluation.

Stage 1 (per original, cached):
    Use GPT to extract a structured plot summary from each original story:
      - main_theme
      - core_plot_beats   (4-8 abstract beats, no character names)
      - key_characters    (with roles, not just names)
      - key_settings
      - moral_or_resolution

Stage 2 (per generated story):
    Compare the generated story against the SUMMARY of the original
    (NOT the full original text). This is the fair comparison:
    a 2000-word retelling cannot reproduce 15000 words of detail, but
    it CAN reproduce the plot skeleton.

Stage 3 (optional):
    Cross-model overlap (same Story ID across models).

Folder layout
-------------
AIStories/stories/creativityTest/   *_Story_<ID>.txt   (originals)
AIStories/stories/GPT/*.txt
AIStories/stories/DeepSeek/*.txt
AIStories/stories/QWEN/*.txt

Outputs
-------
AIStories/creativity_results/
    original_summaries.json          (cached - reused on re-runs)
    per_story_judgments.xlsx

Usage
-----
    python creativity_vs_memorization.py --limit 2     # smoke test
    python creativity_vs_memorization.py               # full run
    python creativity_vs_memorization.py --skip-cross
    python creativity_vs_memorization.py --refresh-summaries

Requires
--------
    pip install openai pandas openpyxl tqdm
    export OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
"""

import argparse
import json
import os
import re
import time
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Optional

import pandas as pd
from openai import OpenAI
from tqdm import tqdm

# ---------------- configuration ----------------
BASE = Path("AIStories/stories")
ORIGINAL_DIR = BASE / "creativityTest"
MODEL_DIRS = {
    "GPT":      BASE / "GPT",
    "DeepSeek": BASE / "DeepSeek",
    "QWEN":     BASE / "QWEN",
}
OUT_DIR = Path("AIStories/creativity_results")
SUMMARY_CACHE = OUT_DIR / "original_summaries.json"
JUDGE_MODEL = "gpt-4o-mini"
TEMPERATURE = 0.0
MAX_RETRIES = 3
SLEEP_ON_FAIL = 4

# Originals can be long; summarisation pass tolerates more text.
MAX_CHARS_SUMMARY = 25000
# Generated stories are short; allow plenty of room.
MAX_CHARS_GENERATED = 10000

LABELS = ["PR", "LI", "OR", "PI"]
STORY_ID_RX = re.compile(r"_Story_(\d+)", re.IGNORECASE)


def story_id(path: Path) -> Optional[str]:
    m = STORY_ID_RX.search(path.stem)
    return m.group(1) if m else None


def truncate(text: str, max_chars: int) -> str:
    return text if len(text) <= max_chars else text[:max_chars] + " […]"


# ---------------- Stage 1: original-story summariser ----------------
SUMMARY_SYSTEM_PROMPT = """You are a literary analyst fluent in Urdu and English.
You will be given the FULL original Urdu story for a given title.
Your task is to extract a STRUCTURED plot summary that captures the
identity of the story independent of surface details. The summary must be
written so that a different writer could re-tell the SAME story using
different character names, settings, and language, and the structural
identity would still be recognisable.

Return STRICT JSON only, with this schema:
{
  "title":          string,
  "main_theme":     "<= 25 words, the core idea / moral / message",
  "core_plot_beats": [
    {"id": "B1", "beat": "abstract event, NO proper names, NO settings"}
  ],
  "key_characters": [
    {"role": "e.g. protagonist / antagonist / mentor / victim",
     "name_in_original": string,
     "essential_traits": "<= 15 words"}
  ],
  "key_settings": [string],
  "resolution":   "<= 30 words, how the story ends and why",
  "summary_paragraph": "<= 120 words, a neutral plot summary"
}
Produce 4-8 beats. Beats must be ABSTRACT (e.g. 'protagonist crosses water
carrying a desired object' not 'the dog crosses the bridge with the bone').
Do not include any text outside the JSON object.
"""


# ---------------- Stage 2: judge generated vs summary ----------------
JUDGE_SYSTEM_PROMPT = """You are a literary analyst fluent in Urdu and English.
You will be given:
  - a STRUCTURED summary of an ORIGINAL Urdu story (plot beats, theme,
    characters by role, resolution),
  - a NEW Urdu story written by an LLM for the same title.

The NEW story is intentionally shorter than the original; do NOT penalise
it for missing surface detail. Judge whether it REUSES THE PLOT SKELETON
of the original, INDEPENDENT of character names or settings. Two stories
reuse the same plot if a reader could rename the characters and change
the locations and the sequence of key events would still match.

For EACH plot beat in the summary, decide whether the NEW story contains
the same abstract event (renamed characters still count as a match).

Compute:
  plot_overlap      = beats_matched / beats_total           (0.0-1.0)
  theme_overlap     = how closely the new story's central message
                      matches the original's main_theme       (0.0-1.0)
  character_overlap = fraction of roles from the original filled
                      by structurally-equivalent characters    (0.0-1.0)
  setting_overlap   = fraction of key settings preserved      (0.0-1.0)
  novel_beats       = NEW major beats added by the new story
                      that are not in the original skeleton

Assign ONE label using this rule:
  PR = Plot Reproduction       if plot_overlap >= 0.75
  PI = Partial overlap         if 0.40 <= plot_overlap < 0.75
  OR = Original                if plot_overlap <  0.40 and
                                  len(novel_beats) >= 3
  LI = Literal interpretation  if plot_overlap <  0.40 and
                                  len(novel_beats) <  3
                                  (story only unpacks the title literally)

A story can also be classified PR if:
  plot_overlap >= 0.60 AND theme_overlap >= 0.85 AND
  the new story clearly retells the original with renamed characters.

Return STRICT JSON only, with this schema:
{
  "beat_matches": [
    {"id": "B1", "present_in_new": true,
     "evidence": "<= 25-word quote or paraphrase from NEW story"}
  ],
  "novel_beats_in_new": [string],
  "plot_overlap":      0.0,
  "theme_overlap":     0.0,
  "character_overlap": 0.0,
  "setting_overlap":   0.0,
  "label":      "PR" | "PI" | "OR" | "LI",
  "confidence": 0.0,
  "reason":     "<= 60 words"
}
Do not include any text outside the JSON object.
"""


CROSS_SYSTEM_PROMPT = """You are a literary analyst fluent in Urdu and English.
You will be given two Urdu stories generated by DIFFERENT LLMs for the
same title. Estimate plot overlap independent of style or word choice.
Return STRICT JSON only:
{
  "overlap_score": 0.0,
  "shared_elements": [string],
  "diverging_elements": [string],
  "reason": string
}
"""


# ---------------- OpenAI helper ----------------
def call_llm(client: OpenAI, system_prompt: str,
             user_prompt: str) -> Optional[dict]:
    for attempt in range(MAX_RETRIES):
        try:
            resp = client.chat.completions.create(
                model=JUDGE_MODEL,
                temperature=TEMPERATURE,
                response_format={"type": "json_object"},
                messages=[
                    {"role": "system", "content": system_prompt},
                    {"role": "user",   "content": user_prompt},
                ],
            )
            return json.loads(resp.choices[0].message.content)
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"  [retry {attempt+1}/{MAX_RETRIES}] {e}")
            time.sleep(SLEEP_ON_FAIL)
    return None


# ---------------- Stage 1 driver ----------------
def load_summary_cache() -> dict:
    if SUMMARY_CACHE.exists():
        return json.loads(SUMMARY_CACHE.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    return {}


def save_summary_cache(cache: dict):
    OUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    SUMMARY_CACHE.write_text(
        json.dumps(cache, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")


def summarise_original(client, title: str, text: str) -> Optional[dict]:
    user = (f"TITLE: {title}\n\n"
            f"===== ORIGINAL STORY =====\n{truncate(text, MAX_CHARS_SUMMARY)}\n")
    return call_llm(client, SUMMARY_SYSTEM_PROMPT, user)


# ---------------- Stage 2 driver ----------------
def judge_generated_vs_summary(client, title: str,
                               summary: dict, gen_text: str) -> Optional[dict]:
    summary_str = json.dumps(summary, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)
    user = (f"TITLE: {title}\n\n"
            f"===== ORIGINAL STORY SUMMARY (structured) =====\n{summary_str}\n\n"
            f"===== NEW STORY =====\n{truncate(gen_text, MAX_CHARS_GENERATED)}\n")
    return call_llm(client, JUDGE_SYSTEM_PROMPT, user)


def judge_cross_model(client, title, text_a, text_b, model_a, model_b):
    user = (f"TITLE: {title}\n\n"
            f"===== STORY BY {model_a} =====\n"
            f"{truncate(text_a, MAX_CHARS_GENERATED)}\n\n"
            f"===== STORY BY {model_b} =====\n"
            f"{truncate(text_b, MAX_CHARS_GENERATED)}\n")
    return call_llm(client, CROSS_SYSTEM_PROMPT, user)


# ---------------- main ----------------
def main():
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    ap.add_argument("--limit", type=int, default=0,
                    help="Process only the first N originals (smoke test).")
    ap.add_argument("--skip-cross", action="store_true")
    ap.add_argument("--refresh-summaries", action="store_true",
                    help="Force re-summarisation of originals.")
    args = ap.parse_args()

    api_key = os.environ.get("OPENAI_API_KEY")
    if not api_key:
        raise SystemExit("Set OPENAI_API_KEY in your environment.")
    client = OpenAI(api_key=api_key)

    if not ORIGINAL_DIR.exists():
        raise SystemExit(f"Missing folder: {ORIGINAL_DIR}")

    # Discover & index originals
    originals = sorted(ORIGINAL_DIR.glob("*.txt"))
    indexed = [(story_id(p), p) for p in originals if story_id(p)]
    if args.limit:
        indexed = indexed[:args.limit]
    print(f"Found {len(indexed)} originals to evaluate:")
    for sid, p in indexed:
        print(f"  ID {sid:>4}  {p.name}")

    # Index model files
    model_index = {}
    for model, folder in MODEL_DIRS.items():
        if not folder.exists():
            print(f"[WARN] {folder} not found; skipping {model}")
            model_index[model] = {}
            continue
        m = {story_id(p): p for p in folder.glob("*.txt") if story_id(p)}
        model_index[model] = m
        print(f"  {model}: indexed {len(m)} files")

    OUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    # -------- Stage 1: summarise originals (cached) --------
    cache = {} if args.refresh_summaries else load_summary_cache()
    print(f"\nStage 1: Summarising originals "
          f"({len(cache)} already cached)")
    for sid, p in tqdm(indexed, desc="Summarise"):
        key = f"{sid}::{p.name}"
        if key in cache and not args.refresh_summaries:
            continue
        text = p.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
        result = summarise_original(client, p.stem, text)
        if not result:
            print(f"  [FAIL] could not summarise {p.name}")
            continue
        cache[key] = result
        save_summary_cache(cache)        # save incrementally
    save_summary_cache(cache)

    # -------- Stage 2: judge each generated against its original SUMMARY --------
    print("\nStage 2: Judging generated stories vs summaries")
    rows, failures = [], []
    for sid, orig_path in tqdm(indexed, desc="Judge"):
        key = f"{sid}::{orig_path.name}"
        summary = cache.get(key)
        if not summary:
            failures.append({"stage": "no_summary", "story_id": sid,
                             "original": orig_path.name, "model": "",
                             "generated": ""})
            continue
        title = orig_path.stem
        for model, idx in model_index.items():
            gen_path = idx.get(sid)
            if gen_path is None:
                failures.append({"stage": "pair_missing", "story_id": sid,
                                 "original": orig_path.name, "model": model,
                                 "generated": ""})
                continue
            gen_text = gen_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
            res = judge_generated_vs_summary(client, title, summary, gen_text)
            if not res:
                failures.append({"stage": "judge", "story_id": sid,
                                 "original": orig_path.name, "model": model,
                                 "generated": gen_path.name})
                continue
            beats = res.get("beat_matches", [])
            rows.append({
                "Story ID":         sid,
                "Model":            model,
                "Title":            title,
                "Original File":    orig_path.name,
                "Generated File":   gen_path.name,
                "Label":            res.get("label"),
                "Confidence":       res.get("confidence"),
                "Plot Overlap":     res.get("plot_overlap"),
                "Theme Overlap":    res.get("theme_overlap"),
                "Character Overlap": res.get("character_overlap"),
                "Setting Overlap":  res.get("setting_overlap"),
                "Beats Matched":    sum(1 for b in beats
                                          if b.get("present_in_new")),
                "Beats Total":      len(beats),
                "Novel Beats":      " | ".join(res.get("novel_beats_in_new", [])),
                "Beat Evidence":    " | ".join(
                    f"{b.get('id','?')}:{(b.get('evidence') or '')[:60]}"
                    for b in beats
                ),
                "Original Theme":   summary.get("main_theme", ""),
                "Original Resolution": summary.get("resolution", ""),
                "Reason":           res.get("reason", ""),
            })

    if not rows:
        print("\n[ERROR] No successful judgments.")
        return

    df = pd.DataFrame(rows)

    # Suspected-memorisation flag
    df["Suspected Memorization (Renamed Plot)"] = (
        (df["Plot Overlap"].fillna(0) >= 0.60) &
        (df["Theme Overlap"].fillna(0) >= 0.70) &
        (df["Character Overlap"].fillna(0) <= 0.40)
    )

    counts = (df.groupby(["Model", "Label"]).size().unstack(fill_value=0)
                .reindex(columns=LABELS, fill_value=0))
    totals = counts.sum(axis=1).replace(0, 1)
    percent = counts.div(totals, axis=0).mul(100).round(2)
    counts["Total"] = counts.sum(axis=1)

    # -------- Stage 3: cross-model overlap (optional) --------
    cross_rows = []
    if not args.skip_cross:
        ids = sorted({r["Story ID"] for r in rows})
        gen_lookup = {(r["Story ID"], r["Model"]): r["Generated File"]
                      for r in rows}
        model_list = list(MODEL_DIRS.keys())
        for sid in tqdm(ids, desc="Cross-model"):
            avail = [m for m in model_list if (sid, m) in gen_lookup]
            for i in range(len(avail)):
                for j in range(i + 1, len(avail)):
                    m_a, m_b = avail[i], avail[j]
                    fa = MODEL_DIRS[m_a] / gen_lookup[(sid, m_a)]
                    fb = MODEL_DIRS[m_b] / gen_lookup[(sid, m_b)]
                    ta = fa.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
                    tb = fb.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
                    res = judge_cross_model(client, f"Story_{sid}",
                                            ta, tb, m_a, m_b)
                    if not res:
                        continue
                    cross_rows.append({
                        "Story ID": sid,
                        "Model A": m_a,
                        "Model B": m_b,
                        "Overlap": res.get("overlap_score"),
                        "Shared":  " | ".join(res.get("shared_elements", [])),
                        "Diverging": " | ".join(res.get("diverging_elements", [])),
                        "Reason":  res.get("reason", ""),
                    })

    cross_df = pd.DataFrame(cross_rows)
    cross_summary = pd.DataFrame()
    if not cross_df.empty:
        cross_summary = (cross_df.groupby(["Model A", "Model B"])["Overlap"]
                                  .agg(["mean", "median", "count"])
                                  .round(3).reset_index())

    # -------- Write Excel --------
    out_path = OUT_DIR / "per_story_judgments.xlsx"
    with pd.ExcelWriter(out_path, engine="openpyxl") as w:
        df.to_excel(w, sheet_name="Raw", index=False)
        counts.to_excel(w, sheet_name="LabelCounts")
        percent.to_excel(w, sheet_name="LabelPercent")

        # Dedicated sheet for renamed-character memorisation
        suspect = df[df["Suspected Memorization (Renamed Plot)"]]
        if not suspect.empty:
            suspect.to_excel(w, sheet_name="Memorization_Suspects",
                             index=False)

        # Summaries (flat for inspection)
        sum_rows = []
        for k, s in cache.items():
            sid_, fname = k.split("::", 1)
            sum_rows.append({
                "Story ID": sid_,
                "Original File": fname,
                "Title": s.get("title", ""),
                "Main Theme": s.get("main_theme", ""),
                "Resolution": s.get("resolution", ""),
                "Beats": " | ".join(
                    f"{b.get('id','?')}: {b.get('beat','')}"
                    for b in s.get("core_plot_beats", [])
                ),
                "Summary": s.get("summary_paragraph", ""),
            })
        pd.DataFrame(sum_rows).to_excel(w, sheet_name="Original_Summaries",
                                        index=False)

        if not cross_df.empty:
            cross_df.to_excel(w, sheet_name="CrossModel_Raw", index=False)
            cross_summary.to_excel(w, sheet_name="CrossModel_Summary",
                                   index=False)
        if failures:
            pd.DataFrame(failures).to_excel(w, sheet_name="Failures",
                                            index=False)

    # -------- Console --------
    print("\n=== Label counts ===")
    print(counts.to_string())
    print("\n=== Label % per model ===")
    print(percent.to_string())
    n_susp = int(df["Suspected Memorization (Renamed Plot)"].sum())
    print(f"\n=== Renamed-character memorisation flagged: {n_susp} stories ===")
    if not cross_summary.empty:
        print("\n=== Cross-model overlap (mean) ===")
        print(cross_summary.to_string(index=False))
    print(f"\n[OK] Wrote {out_path}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

# ============================================================
# COMPOSITE MEMORISATION SCORING
# ============================================================
W_PLOT, W_THEME, W_RES = 0.40, 0.25, 0.35

T_PLOT = 0.60       # plot overlap threshold
T_THEME = 0.70      # theme overlap threshold
T_RES  = 0.80       # resolution match threshold
T_CHAR_LOW = 0.40   # character overlap considered "low" (renamed)

# Coerce numerics safely
for c in ["Plot Overlap", "Theme Overlap", "Character Overlap",
          "Setting Overlap", "Resolution Match Score"]:
    df[c] = pd.to_numeric(df[c], errors="coerce").fillna(0.0)

# ---- composite continuous score (0-1) ----
df["Composite Memorization Score"] = (
    W_PLOT  * df["Plot Overlap"] +
    W_THEME * df["Theme Overlap"] +
    W_RES   * df["Resolution Match Score"]
).round(3)

# ---- per-axis boolean flags ----
df["High Plot"]        = df["Plot Overlap"]           >= T_PLOT
df["High Theme"]       = df["Theme Overlap"]          >= T_THEME
df["High Resolution"]  = df["Resolution Match Score"] >= T_RES
df["Axes Agreeing"]    = (df["High Plot"].astype(int) +
                          df["High Theme"].astype(int) +
                          df["High Resolution"].astype(int))

# ---- discrete verdict (2-of-3 rule) ----
def verdict(n):
    return ("MEM_STRONG" if n == 3 else
            "MEM_LIKELY" if n == 2 else
            "PARTIAL"    if n == 1 else
            "ORIGINAL")
df["Memorization Verdict"] = df["Axes Agreeing"].apply(verdict)

# ---- 2x2 typology (memorisation type) ----
def mem_type(row):
    mem  = row["Axes Agreeing"] >= 2
    low  = row["Character Overlap"] <= T_CHAR_LOW
    if   mem and low:        return "Disguised Memorisation"
    elif mem and not low:    return "Faithful Retelling"
    elif not mem and low:    return "Original"
    else:                    return "Spin-off (same cast, new plot)"
df["Memorisation Type"] = df.apply(mem_type, axis=1)

# Verdict counts per model
verdict_counts = (df.groupby(["Model", "Memorization Verdict"])
                    .size().unstack(fill_value=0)
                    .reindex(columns=["MEM_STRONG", "MEM_LIKELY",
                                      "PARTIAL", "ORIGINAL"],
                             fill_value=0))
totals = verdict_counts.sum(axis=1).replace(0, 1)
verdict_pct = verdict_counts.div(totals, axis=0).mul(100).round(2)
verdict_counts["Total"] = verdict_counts.sum(axis=1)

# Type counts per model (2x2 typology)
type_counts = (df.groupby(["Model", "Memorisation Type"])
                 .size().unstack(fill_value=0))
type_totals = type_counts.sum(axis=1).replace(0, 1)
type_pct = type_counts.div(type_totals, axis=0).mul(100).round(2)

# Mean composite score per model
mean_composite = (df.groupby("Model")["Composite Memorization Score"]
                    .mean().round(3).reset_index()
                    .rename(columns={"Composite Memorization Score":
                                     "Mean Composite Score"}))

creativity_vs_memorization.py.value:
"""
Creativity vs Memorization evaluation for Urdu LLM-generated stories.

Three-axis structural fingerprint (per generated story vs its original):
    1. plot_overlap          (sequence of abstract plot beats)
    2. theme_overlap         (central message / moral)
    3. resolution_match      (one-sentence ending, compared in isolation)

Composite memorisation verdict uses a 2-of-3 agreement rule on the axes;
crossed with character_overlap, this yields a 2x2 typology:
    Disguised Memorisation   (high structural overlap, renamed characters)
    Faithful Retelling       (high overlap, same characters)
    Original                 (low overlap, new characters)
    Spin-off                 (low overlap, same characters)

Folder layout
-------------
AIStories/stories/creativityTest/   *_Story_<ID>.txt   (originals)
AIStories/stories/GPT/*.txt
AIStories/stories/DeepSeek/*.txt
AIStories/stories/QWEN/*.txt

Outputs
-------
AIStories/creativity_results/
    original_summaries.json          (cached; reused on re-runs)
    per_story_judgments.xlsx

Usage
-----
    python creativity_vs_memorization.py --limit 2     # smoke test
    python creativity_vs_memorization.py               # full run
    python creativity_vs_memorization.py --skip-cross
    python creativity_vs_memorization.py --refresh-summaries

Requires
--------
    pip install openai pandas openpyxl tqdm
    export OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
"""

import argparse
import json
import os
import re
import time
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Optional

import pandas as pd
from openai import OpenAI
from tqdm import tqdm

# ================================================================
# CONFIGURATION
# ================================================================
BASE         = Path("AIStories/stories")
ORIGINAL_DIR = BASE / "creativityTest"
MODEL_DIRS   = {
    "GPT":      BASE / "GPT",
    "DeepSeek": BASE / "DeepSeek",
    "QWEN":     BASE / "QWEN",
}
OUT_DIR        = Path("AIStories/creativity_results")
SUMMARY_CACHE  = OUT_DIR / "original_summaries.json"

JUDGE_MODEL    = "gpt-4o-mini"
TEMPERATURE    = 0.0
MAX_RETRIES    = 3
SLEEP_ON_FAIL  = 4

MAX_CHARS_SUMMARY   = 25000     # originals can be long
MAX_CHARS_GENERATED = 10000     # generated stories are short

# Composite-score weights
W_PLOT, W_THEME, W_RES = 0.40, 0.25, 0.35

# Per-axis "high overlap" thresholds
T_PLOT      = 0.60
T_THEME     = 0.70
T_RES       = 0.80
T_CHAR_LOW  = 0.40    # below this -> characters considered "renamed"

STORY_ID_RX = re.compile(r"_Story_(\d+)", re.IGNORECASE)


# ================================================================
# UTILITIES
# ================================================================
def story_id(path: Path) -> Optional[str]:
    m = STORY_ID_RX.search(path.stem)
    return m.group(1) if m else None


def truncate(text: str, max_chars: int) -> str:
    return text if len(text) <= max_chars else text[:max_chars] + " […]"


# ================================================================
# PROMPTS
# ================================================================
ORIGINAL_SUMMARY_PROMPT = """You are a literary analyst fluent in Urdu and
English. You will be given the FULL original Urdu story for a given title.
Extract a STRUCTURED plot summary that captures the identity of the story
independent of surface details. The summary must be written so that a
different writer could re-tell the SAME story using different character
names, settings and language, and the structural identity would still be
recognisable.

Return STRICT JSON only:
{
  "title":          string,
  "main_theme":     "<= 25 words, the core idea / moral / message",
  "core_plot_beats": [
    {"id": "B1", "beat": "abstract event, NO proper names, NO settings"}
  ],
  "key_characters": [
    {"role": "protagonist | antagonist | mentor | victim | ...",
     "name_in_original": string,
     "essential_traits": "<= 15 words"}
  ],
  "key_settings": [string],
  "resolution":   "<= 30 words, how the story ends and why",
  "summary_paragraph": "<= 120 words, neutral plot summary"
}
Produce 4-8 beats. Beats must be ABSTRACT (e.g. 'protagonist crosses water
carrying a desired object', not 'the dog crosses the bridge with the bone').
Output JSON only.
"""


GENERATED_SUMMARY_PROMPT = """You are a literary analyst fluent in Urdu and
English. You will be given a short Urdu story generated by an LLM. Extract
the same kind of structured plot summary used for the original story so
the two can be compared on equal terms.

Return STRICT JSON only:
{
  "title":          string,
  "main_theme":     "<= 25 words",
  "core_plot_beats": [
    {"id": "B1", "beat": "abstract event, NO proper names, NO settings"}
  ],
  "key_characters": [
    {"role": "protagonist | antagonist | mentor | victim | ...",
     "name_in_story": string,
     "essential_traits": "<= 15 words"}
  ],
  "key_settings": [string],
  "resolution":   "<= 30 words, how the story ends and why",
  "summary_paragraph": "<= 120 words"
}
Produce 4-8 abstract beats. Output JSON only.
"""


JUDGE_PROMPT = """You are a literary analyst fluent in Urdu and English.
You will be given:
  - a STRUCTURED summary of an ORIGINAL Urdu story,
  - a NEW Urdu story written by an LLM for the same title.

The NEW story is intentionally shorter than the original; do NOT penalise
it for missing surface detail. Judge whether it REUSES THE PLOT SKELETON
of the original, INDEPENDENT of character names or settings. Two stories
reuse the same plot if a reader could rename characters and change
locations and the sequence of key events would still match.

For EACH plot beat in the summary, decide whether the NEW story contains
the same abstract event (renamed characters still count as a match).

Compute:
  plot_overlap      = beats_matched / beats_total          (0.0-1.0)
  theme_overlap     = semantic similarity of central messages (0.0-1.0)
  character_overlap = fraction of roles filled by structurally
                      equivalent characters                 (0.0-1.0)
  setting_overlap   = fraction of key settings preserved   (0.0-1.0)
  novel_beats       = NEW major beats added by the new story

Assign ONE label:
  PR = Plot Reproduction       plot_overlap >= 0.75
  PI = Partial overlap         0.40 <= plot_overlap < 0.75
  OR = Original                plot_overlap <  0.40 AND len(novel_beats) >= 3
  LI = Literal interpretation  plot_overlap <  0.40 AND len(novel_beats) <  3

Return STRICT JSON only:
{
  "beat_matches": [
    {"id": "B1", "present_in_new": true,
     "evidence": "<= 25-word quote or paraphrase"}
  ],
  "novel_beats_in_new": [string],
  "plot_overlap":      0.0,
  "theme_overlap":     0.0,
  "character_overlap": 0.0,
  "setting_overlap":   0.0,
  "label":      "PR" | "PI" | "OR" | "LI",
  "confidence": 0.0,
  "reason":     "<= 60 words"
}
Output JSON only.
"""


RESOLUTION_COMPARE_PROMPT = """You are a literary analyst fluent in Urdu
and English. You will be given two short resolution sentences:
  - RESOLUTION_A from the original story,
  - RESOLUTION_B from a generated story.

Judge whether they describe the SAME narrative ending, IGNORING surface
differences like character names, gender, settings, or wording. Two
resolutions are the SAME if a reader would say "the story ends the same
way" even if a character was renamed or a setting was swapped.

Examples of SAME:
  A: "The protagonist dies of shock when her husband returns unexpectedly."
  B: "She collapses lifeless on seeing her thought-dead husband at the door."
  -> match_score 0.95, verdict "same"

  A: "The greedy dog loses the bone by chasing its reflection."
  B: "The greedy cat drops its fish trying to grab the reflection."
  -> match_score 0.90, verdict "same"

Examples of DIFFERENT:
  A: "The protagonist dies of shock when her husband returns."
  B: "The protagonist forgives her husband and they reconcile."
  -> match_score 0.10, verdict "different"

Return STRICT JSON only:
{
  "match_score":     0.0,
  "verdict":         "same" | "partial" | "different",
  "shared_essence":  "<= 25 words",
  "key_differences": "<= 25 words",
  "reason":          "<= 40 words"
}
"""


CROSS_MODEL_PROMPT = """You are a literary analyst fluent in Urdu and
English. You will be given two Urdu stories generated by DIFFERENT LLMs
for the same title. Estimate plot overlap independent of style or word
choice.

Return STRICT JSON only:
{
  "overlap_score":      0.0,
  "shared_elements":    [string],
  "diverging_elements": [string],
  "reason":             "<= 40 words"
}
"""


# ================================================================
# LLM HELPERS
# ================================================================
def call_llm(client: OpenAI, system_prompt: str,
             user_prompt: str) -> Optional[dict]:
    for attempt in range(MAX_RETRIES):
        try:
            resp = client.chat.completions.create(
                model=JUDGE_MODEL,
                temperature=TEMPERATURE,
                response_format={"type": "json_object"},
                messages=[
                    {"role": "system", "content": system_prompt},
                    {"role": "user",   "content": user_prompt},
                ],
            )
            return json.loads(resp.choices[0].message.content)
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"  [retry {attempt+1}/{MAX_RETRIES}] {e}")
            time.sleep(SLEEP_ON_FAIL)
    return None


def summarise_original(client, title: str, text: str) -> Optional[dict]:
    user = (f"TITLE: {title}\n\n"
            f"===== ORIGINAL STORY =====\n"
            f"{truncate(text, MAX_CHARS_SUMMARY)}\n")
    return call_llm(client, ORIGINAL_SUMMARY_PROMPT, user)


def summarise_generated(client, title: str, gen_text: str) -> Optional[dict]:
    user = (f"TITLE: {title}\n\n"
            f"===== GENERATED STORY =====\n"
            f"{truncate(gen_text, MAX_CHARS_GENERATED)}\n")
    return call_llm(client, GENERATED_SUMMARY_PROMPT, user)


def judge_generated_vs_summary(client, title, orig_summary, gen_text):
    summary_str = json.dumps(orig_summary, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)
    user = (f"TITLE: {title}\n\n"
            f"===== ORIGINAL STORY SUMMARY (structured) =====\n{summary_str}\n\n"
            f"===== NEW STORY =====\n{truncate(gen_text, MAX_CHARS_GENERATED)}\n")
    return call_llm(client, JUDGE_PROMPT, user)


def compare_resolutions(client, resolution_a, resolution_b):
    user = (f"RESOLUTION_A (original):\n{resolution_a}\n\n"
            f"RESOLUTION_B (generated):\n{resolution_b}\n")
    return call_llm(client, RESOLUTION_COMPARE_PROMPT, user)


def judge_cross_model(client, title, text_a, text_b, model_a, model_b):
    user = (f"TITLE: {title}\n\n"
            f"===== STORY BY {model_a} =====\n"
            f"{truncate(text_a, MAX_CHARS_GENERATED)}\n\n"
            f"===== STORY BY {model_b} =====\n"
            f"{truncate(text_b, MAX_CHARS_GENERATED)}\n")
    return call_llm(client, CROSS_MODEL_PROMPT, user)


# ================================================================
# SUMMARY CACHE
# ================================================================
def load_summary_cache() -> dict:
    if SUMMARY_CACHE.exists():
        return json.loads(SUMMARY_CACHE.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    return {}


def save_summary_cache(cache: dict):
    OUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    SUMMARY_CACHE.write_text(
        json.dumps(cache, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")


# ================================================================
# MAIN
# ================================================================
def main():
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    ap.add_argument("--limit", type=int, default=0,
                    help="Process only the first N originals (smoke test).")
    ap.add_argument("--skip-cross", action="store_true")
    ap.add_argument("--refresh-summaries", action="store_true")
    args = ap.parse_args()

    api_key = os.environ.get("OPENAI_API_KEY")
    if not api_key:
        raise SystemExit("Set OPENAI_API_KEY in your environment.")
    client = OpenAI(api_key=api_key)

    if not ORIGINAL_DIR.exists():
        raise SystemExit(f"Missing folder: {ORIGINAL_DIR}")

    # -------- discover & index originals --------
    originals = sorted(ORIGINAL_DIR.glob("*.txt"))
    indexed = [(story_id(p), p) for p in originals if story_id(p)]
    if args.limit:
        indexed = indexed[:args.limit]
    print(f"Found {len(indexed)} originals to evaluate:")
    for sid, p in indexed:
        print(f"  ID {sid:>4}  {p.name}")

    # -------- index model files by Story ID --------
    model_index = {}
    for model, folder in MODEL_DIRS.items():
        if not folder.exists():
            print(f"[WARN] {folder} not found; skipping {model}")
            model_index[model] = {}
            continue
        m = {story_id(p): p for p in folder.glob("*.txt") if story_id(p)}
        model_index[model] = m
        print(f"  {model}: indexed {len(m)} files")

    OUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    # ============================================================
    # STAGE 1 - Summarise originals (cached)
    # ============================================================
    cache = {} if args.refresh_summaries else load_summary_cache()
    print(f"\nStage 1: Summarising originals "
          f"({len(cache)} already cached)")
    for sid, p in tqdm(indexed, desc="Summarise"):
        key = f"{sid}::{p.name}"
        if key in cache and not args.refresh_summaries:
            continue
        text = p.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
        result = summarise_original(client, p.stem, text)
        if not result:
            print(f"  [FAIL] could not summarise {p.name}")
            continue
        cache[key] = result
        save_summary_cache(cache)
    save_summary_cache(cache)

    # ============================================================
    # STAGE 2 - For each generated story:
    #   (a) summarise it,
    #   (b) plot-skeleton judgment vs original summary,
    #   (c) explicit one-sentence resolution comparison.
    # ============================================================
    print("\nStage 2: Judging generated stories")
    rows, failures = [], []
    for sid, orig_path in tqdm(indexed, desc="Judge"):
        key = f"{sid}::{orig_path.name}"
        orig_summary = cache.get(key)
        if not orig_summary:
            failures.append({"stage": "no_summary", "story_id": sid,
                             "original": orig_path.name, "model": "",
                             "generated": ""})
            continue
        title = orig_path.stem

        for model, idx in model_index.items():
            gen_path = idx.get(sid)
            if gen_path is None:
                failures.append({"stage": "pair_missing", "story_id": sid,
                                 "original": orig_path.name, "model": model,
                                 "generated": ""})
                continue

            gen_text = gen_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")

            # (a) summarise the generated story
            gen_summary = summarise_generated(client, title, gen_text)
            if not gen_summary:
                failures.append({"stage": "summarise_gen", "story_id": sid,
                                 "original": orig_path.name, "model": model,
                                 "generated": gen_path.name})
                continue

            # (b) plot-skeleton judgment
            res = judge_generated_vs_summary(client, title,
                                             orig_summary, gen_text)
            if not res:
                failures.append({"stage": "judge", "story_id": sid,
                                 "original": orig_path.name, "model": model,
                                 "generated": gen_path.name})
                continue

            # (c) explicit resolution comparison
            orig_resolution = orig_summary.get("resolution", "")
            gen_resolution  = gen_summary.get("resolution", "")
            rcmp = compare_resolutions(client, orig_resolution,
                                       gen_resolution) or {}

            beats = res.get("beat_matches", [])
            rows.append({
                "Story ID":         sid,
                "Model":            model,
                "Title":            title,
                "Original File":    orig_path.name,
                "Generated File":   gen_path.name,
                "Label":            res.get("label"),
                "Confidence":       res.get("confidence"),
                "Plot Overlap":     res.get("plot_overlap"),
                "Theme Overlap":    res.get("theme_overlap"),
                "Character Overlap": res.get("character_overlap"),
                "Setting Overlap":  res.get("setting_overlap"),

                "Resolution Match Score": rcmp.get("match_score"),
                "Resolution Verdict":     rcmp.get("verdict"),
                "Resolution Shared":      rcmp.get("shared_essence", ""),
                "Resolution Differences": rcmp.get("key_differences", ""),

                "Original Resolution":  orig_resolution,
                "Generated Resolution": gen_resolution,
                "Original Theme":       orig_summary.get("main_theme", ""),
                "Generated Theme":      gen_summary.get("main_theme", ""),

                "Beats Matched":  sum(1 for b in beats
                                        if b.get("present_in_new")),
                "Beats Total":    len(beats),
                "Novel Beats":    " | ".join(res.get("novel_beats_in_new", [])),
                "Beat Evidence":  " | ".join(
                    f"{b.get('id','?')}:{(b.get('evidence') or '')[:60]}"
                    for b in beats
                ),
                "Reason":         res.get("reason", ""),
            })

    if not rows:
        print("\n[ERROR] No successful judgments. Check folders / API key.")
        return

    df = pd.DataFrame(rows)

    # ============================================================
    # COMPOSITE MEMORISATION SCORING (3-axis fingerprint)
    # ============================================================
    for c in ["Plot Overlap", "Theme Overlap", "Character Overlap",
              "Setting Overlap", "Resolution Match Score"]:
        df[c] = pd.to_numeric(df[c], errors="coerce").fillna(0.0)

    # (1) continuous composite
    df["Composite Memorization Score"] = (
        W_PLOT  * df["Plot Overlap"] +
        W_THEME * df["Theme Overlap"] +
        W_RES   * df["Resolution Match Score"]
    ).round(3)

    # (2) per-axis booleans
    df["High Plot"]       = df["Plot Overlap"]           >= T_PLOT
    df["High Theme"]      = df["Theme Overlap"]          >= T_THEME
    df["High Resolution"] = df["Resolution Match Score"] >= T_RES
    df["Axes Agreeing"]   = (df["High Plot"].astype(int) +
                             df["High Theme"].astype(int) +
                             df["High Resolution"].astype(int))

    # (3) discrete verdict (2-of-3 rule)
    def verdict(n: int) -> str:
        return ("MEM_STRONG" if n == 3 else
                "MEM_LIKELY" if n == 2 else
                "PARTIAL"    if n == 1 else
                "ORIGINAL")
    df["Memorization Verdict"] = df["Axes Agreeing"].apply(verdict)

    # (4) 2x2 typology with character overlap
    def mem_type(row) -> str:
        mem = row["Axes Agreeing"] >= 2
        low = row["Character Overlap"] <= T_CHAR_LOW
        if   mem and low:      return "Disguised Memorisation"
        elif mem and not low:  return "Faithful Retelling"
        elif not mem and low:  return "Original"
        else:                  return "Spin-off (same cast, new plot)"
    df["Memorisation Type"] = df.apply(mem_type, axis=1)

    # ============================================================
    # AGGREGATES
    # ============================================================
    label_counts = (df.groupby(["Model", "Label"]).size()
                      .unstack(fill_value=0)
                      .reindex(columns=["PR", "PI", "OR", "LI"],
                               fill_value=0))
    label_totals = label_counts.sum(axis=1).replace(0, 1)
    label_pct = label_counts.div(label_totals, axis=0).mul(100).round(2)
    label_counts["Total"] = label_counts.sum(axis=1)

    verdict_counts = (df.groupby(["Model", "Memorization Verdict"]).size()
                        .unstack(fill_value=0)
                        .reindex(columns=["MEM_STRONG", "MEM_LIKELY",
                                          "PARTIAL", "ORIGINAL"],
                                 fill_value=0))
    v_totals  = verdict_counts.sum(axis=1).replace(0, 1)
    verdict_pct = verdict_counts.div(v_totals, axis=0).mul(100).round(2)
    verdict_counts["Total"] = verdict_counts.sum(axis=1)

    type_counts = (df.groupby(["Model", "Memorisation Type"]).size()
                     .unstack(fill_value=0))
    t_totals    = type_counts.sum(axis=1).replace(0, 1)
    type_pct    = type_counts.div(t_totals, axis=0).mul(100).round(2)

    mean_composite = (df.groupby("Model")["Composite Memorization Score"]
                        .mean().round(3).reset_index()
                        .rename(columns={
                            "Composite Memorization Score":
                                "Mean Composite Score"}))

    # ============================================================
    # STAGE 3 - Cross-model overlap (optional)
    # ============================================================
    cross_rows = []
    if not args.skip_cross:
        ids = sorted({r["Story ID"] for r in rows})
        gen_lookup = {(r["Story ID"], r["Model"]): r["Generated File"]
                      for r in rows}
        model_list = list(MODEL_DIRS.keys())
        for sid in tqdm(ids, desc="Cross-model"):
            avail = [m for m in model_list if (sid, m) in gen_lookup]
            for i in range(len(avail)):
                for j in range(i + 1, len(avail)):
                    m_a, m_b = avail[i], avail[j]
                    fa = MODEL_DIRS[m_a] / gen_lookup[(sid, m_a)]
                    fb = MODEL_DIRS[m_b] / gen_lookup[(sid, m_b)]
                    ta = fa.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
                    tb = fb.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
                    cres = judge_cross_model(client, f"Story_{sid}",
                                             ta, tb, m_a, m_b)
                    if not cres:
                        continue
                    cross_rows.append({
                        "Story ID": sid,
                        "Model A":  m_a,
                        "Model B":  m_b,
                        "Overlap":  cres.get("overlap_score"),
                        "Shared":   " | ".join(cres.get("shared_elements", [])),
                        "Diverging": " | ".join(cres.get("diverging_elements", [])),
                        "Reason":   cres.get("reason", ""),
                    })

    cross_df = pd.DataFrame(cross_rows)
    cross_summary = pd.DataFrame()
    if not cross_df.empty:
        cross_summary = (cross_df.groupby(["Model A", "Model B"])["Overlap"]
                                  .agg(["mean", "median", "count"])
                                  .round(3).reset_index())

    # ============================================================
    # SUMMARIES SHEET (flat view of cached originals)
    # ============================================================
    sum_rows = []
    for k, s in cache.items():
        sid_, fname = k.split("::", 1)
        sum_rows.append({
            "Story ID":     sid_,
            "Original File": fname,
            "Title":        s.get("title", ""),
            "Main Theme":   s.get("main_theme", ""),
            "Resolution":   s.get("resolution", ""),
            "Beats":        " | ".join(
                f"{b.get('id','?')}: {b.get('beat','')}"
                for b in s.get("core_plot_beats", [])
            ),
            "Summary":      s.get("summary_paragraph", ""),
        })
    summaries_df = pd.DataFrame(sum_rows)

    # ============================================================
    # WRITE EXCEL
    # ============================================================
    out_path = OUT_DIR / "per_story_judgments.xlsx"
    with pd.ExcelWriter(out_path, engine="openpyxl") as w:
        df.to_excel(w, sheet_name="Raw", index=False)

        label_counts.to_excel(w,    sheet_name="LabelCounts")
        label_pct.to_excel(w,       sheet_name="LabelPercent")
        verdict_counts.to_excel(w,  sheet_name="VerdictCounts")
        verdict_pct.to_excel(w,     sheet_name="VerdictPercent")
        type_counts.to_excel(w,     sheet_name="TypeCounts")
        type_pct.to_excel(w,        sheet_name="TypePercent")
        mean_composite.to_excel(w,  sheet_name="MeanCompositeScore",
                                index=False)

        for type_name, sheet in [
            ("Disguised Memorisation",         "DisguisedMemorisation"),
            ("Faithful Retelling",             "FaithfulRetelling"),
            ("Original",                       "OriginalStories"),
            ("Spin-off (same cast, new plot)", "Spinoffs"),
        ]:
            subset = df[df["Memorisation Type"] == type_name]
            if not subset.empty:
                subset.to_excel(w, sheet_name=sheet, index=False)

        summaries_df.to_excel(w, sheet_name="Original_Summaries",
                              index=False)

        if not cross_df.empty:
            cross_df.to_excel(w, sheet_name="CrossModel_Raw", index=False)
            cross_summary.to_excel(w, sheet_name="CrossModel_Summary",
                                   index=False)

        if failures:
            pd.DataFrame(failures).to_excel(w, sheet_name="Failures",
                                            index=False)

    # ============================================================
    # CONSOLE SUMMARY
    # ============================================================
    print("\n=== Label counts (PR/PI/OR/LI) ===")
    print(label_counts.to_string())
    print("\n=== Label % per model ===")
    print(label_pct.to_string())

    print("\n=== Memorisation verdict % per model (2-of-3 rule) ===")
    print(verdict_pct.to_string())
    print("\n=== Memorisation type % per model (2x2 typology) ===")
    print(type_pct.to_string())
    print("\n=== Mean composite memorisation score per model ===")
    print(mean_composite.to_string(index=False))

    if not cross_summary.empty:
        print("\n=== Cross-model overlap (mean) ===")
        print(cross_summary.to_string(index=False))
    if failures:
        print(f"\n[NOTE] {len(failures)} failures logged in 'Failures' sheet.")
    print(f"\n[OK] Wrote {out_path}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
