"""
Replace SYSTEM_PROMPT and SCHEMA in creativity_vs_memorization.py with this.
Adds character-vs-plot decomposition so 'same plot with renamed characters'
is correctly classified as PR, not PI.
"""

SYSTEM_PROMPT = """You are a literary analyst fluent in Urdu and English.
You will be given:
  - the ORIGINAL Urdu story for a given title,
  - a NEW Urdu story written by an LLM for the same title.

Your task is to judge how much the NEW story REUSES THE PLOT of the
ORIGINAL, INDEPENDENT of character names, settings, or surface details.
Two stories reuse the same plot if a reader could swap their characters,
names and locations and the sequence of key events would still match.

STEP 1 - Extract plot skeleton of the ORIGINAL as 4-8 atomic story beats.
A beat is a single causally-important event, stated abstractly (no names).
Example for the folk tale 'Greedy Dog':
  B1. Protagonist obtains a desired object.
  B2. Protagonist crosses water carrying the object.
  B3. Protagonist sees its own reflection in the water.
  B4. Protagonist mistakes the reflection for another holder of a bigger object.
  B5. Protagonist opens its mouth to grab the bigger object.
  B6. The real object falls and is lost; moral: greed causes loss.

STEP 2 - For EACH beat, check whether the NEW story contains the same
abstract event, regardless of whether characters or settings were renamed.
Output a per-beat present/absent decision.

STEP 3 - Compute:
  plot_overlap     = beats_matched / beats_total      (0.0 - 1.0)
  character_overlap = fraction of named characters/roles from the original
                      that appear with the same role in the new story (0.0-1.0)
  setting_overlap   = fraction of key settings/locations preserved (0.0-1.0)
  novel_beats       = NEW beats added in the new story that are not in
                      the original skeleton

STEP 4 - Assign ONE label using the following decision rule:
  if plot_overlap >= 0.75:                              label = "PR"
  elif plot_overlap >= 0.40:                            label = "PI"
  elif plot_overlap <  0.40 and novel_beats_count >= 3: label = "OR"
  elif plot_overlap <  0.40 and novel_beats_count <  3: label = "LI"

Where:
  PR = Plot Reproduction (same plot skeleton, even if characters renamed)
  PI = Partial overlap (some beats match, others new)
  OR = Original (genuinely new plot)
  LI = Literal interpretation of the title only, with no real plot beyond
       what the title literally says

Return STRICT JSON only, with this schema:
{
  "original_beats": [
    {"id": "B1", "beat": "abstract description without names"}
  ],
  "beat_matches": [
    {"id": "B1", "present_in_new": true,
     "evidence": "short Urdu/English quote or paraphrase from the NEW story"}
  ],
  "novel_beats_in_new": [string],
  "plot_overlap": 0.0,
  "character_overlap": 0.0,
  "setting_overlap": 0.0,
  "label": "PR" | "PI" | "OR" | "LI",
  "confidence": 0.0,
  "reason": "<= 60 words explaining the decision"
}
Do not include any text outside the JSON object.
"""

section_creativity_memorization.tex.name: section_creativity_memorization.tex
section_creativity_memorization.tex.value:
\subsection{Creativity vs Memorization}
\label{subsec:creativity}

To distinguish creative generation from plot reproduction, we developed
a three-axis structural fingerprint. For every original--generated story
pair, GPT-4o-mini (acting as a literary judge with a temperature of 0)
produces an independent score on each axis:
plot overlap (the proportion of the original's abstract plot beats that
recur in the generated story), theme overlap (semantic similarity of the
two stories' central messages), and resolution match (semantic similarity
of the one-sentence narrative resolutions, compared in isolation). Each
score is mapped to a boolean using a fixed threshold
(plot $\ge$ 0.60, theme $\ge$ 0.70, resolution $\ge$ 0.80). A generated
story is classified as memorisation when at least two of the three axes
agree. We further cross this verdict with character overlap to obtain a
2$\times$2 typology: \emph{Disguised Memorisation} (high structural
overlap with renamed characters), \emph{Faithful Retelling} (high
overlap, same characters), \emph{Original} (low overlap, new characters),
and \emph{Spin-off} (low overlap, same characters).

Across the XX evaluated stories, QWEN exhibits the highest disguised
memorisation rate (50\%), followed by DeepSeek (20\%) and GPT (0\%);
GPT is also the most original (80\%). The mean composite memorisation
score, computed as a 0.40/0.25/0.35 weighted combination of the three
axes, follows the same ordering (QWEN 0.70, DeepSeek 0.40, GPT 0.23).
The qualitative pattern is consistent with the linguistic-error profile
of Section~\ref{subsec:linguistic_errors}: QWEN's outputs are
structurally close to memorised source material yet morphologically the
least accurate, suggesting that surface fluency and structural
originality are not jointly optimised by the current generation of
Urdu LLMs.

theme_overlap.py.name: theme_overlap.py
theme_overlap.py.value:
"""
Theme-only Creativity vs Memorization evaluation for Urdu LLM-generated
stories.

For each (original, generated) pair we:
  1. Summarise the original ONCE (cached) and extract its main_theme.
  2. Summarise the generated story and extract its main_theme.
  3. Ask the judge to compare the two themes in isolation and return a
     theme_overlap score (0.0-1.0) plus a verdict (same / partial / different).

Discrete verdict cut-offs:
    theme_overlap >= 0.80  -> THEME_SAME         (memorised theme)
    theme_overlap >= 0.50  -> THEME_PARTIAL
    theme_overlap <  0.50  -> THEME_DIFFERENT    (original theme)

Folder layout
-------------
AIStories/stories/creativityTest/   *_Story_<ID>.txt   (originals)
AIStories/stories/GPT/*.txt
AIStories/stories/DeepSeek/*.txt
AIStories/stories/QWEN/*.txt

Outputs
-------
AIStories/creativity_results/
    original_theme_cache.json
    theme_judgments.xlsx

Usage
-----
    python theme_overlap.py --limit 2     # smoke test
    python theme_overlap.py               # full run
    python theme_overlap.py --refresh-cache

Requires
--------
    pip install openai pandas openpyxl tqdm
    export OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
"""

import argparse
import json
import os
import re
import time
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Optional

import pandas as pd
from openai import OpenAI
from tqdm import tqdm

# ================================================================
# CONFIGURATION
# ================================================================
BASE         = Path("AIStories/stories")
ORIGINAL_DIR = BASE / "creativityTest"
MODEL_DIRS   = {
    "GPT":      BASE / "GPT",
    "DeepSeek": BASE / "DeepSeek",
    "QWEN":     BASE / "QWEN",
}
OUT_DIR      = Path("AIStories/creativity_results")
THEME_CACHE  = OUT_DIR / "original_theme_cache.json"

JUDGE_MODEL    = "gpt-4o-mini"
TEMPERATURE    = 0.0
MAX_RETRIES    = 3
SLEEP_ON_FAIL  = 4

MAX_CHARS_ORIGINAL  = 25000
MAX_CHARS_GENERATED = 10000

T_SAME    = 0.80
T_PARTIAL = 0.50

STORY_ID_RX = re.compile(r"_Story_(\d+)", re.IGNORECASE)


# ================================================================
# UTILITIES
# ================================================================
def story_id(path: Path) -> Optional[str]:
    m = STORY_ID_RX.search(path.stem)
    return m.group(1) if m else None


def truncate(text: str, max_chars: int) -> str:
    return text if len(text) <= max_chars else text[:max_chars] + " […]"


# ================================================================
# PROMPTS
# ================================================================
THEME_EXTRACT_PROMPT = """You are a literary analyst fluent in Urdu and
English. You will be given an Urdu story. Identify its MAIN THEME, that
is, the single core idea, moral, or message the story communicates.
The theme must be stated ABSTRACTLY, without character names or specific
settings, so it could apply to a re-telling with different characters.

Return STRICT JSON only:
{
  "main_theme":       "<= 25 words, the abstract central idea / moral",
  "theme_keywords":   [string],   // 3-6 short keywords
  "supporting_evidence": "<= 30 words, brief justification from the story"
}
Output JSON only.
"""


THEME_COMPARE_PROMPT = """You are a literary analyst fluent in Urdu and
English. You will be given two themes:
  - THEME_A  from the original story,
  - THEME_B  from a generated story.

Judge whether they express the SAME core idea / moral, IGNORING surface
differences (character names, settings, wording). Two themes are the SAME
if a reader would say "both stories teach the same lesson" or "both
stories are fundamentally about the same human experience".

Examples of SAME:
  A: "Greed leads to losing what one already has."
  B: "Excessive desire causes a person to forfeit their existing blessings."
  -> overlap 0.95, verdict "same"

Examples of PARTIAL:
  A: "A woman's brief taste of freedom is destroyed by patriarchy's return."
  B: "Women face limits on their independence in society."
  -> overlap 0.55, verdict "partial"   (B is broader and weaker than A)

Examples of DIFFERENT:
  A: "Greed leads to losing what one already has."
  B: "Hard work and patience are rewarded."
  -> overlap 0.10, verdict "different"

Return STRICT JSON only:
{
  "theme_overlap":   0.0,
  "verdict":         "same" | "partial" | "different",
  "shared_essence":  "<= 25 words",
  "key_differences": "<= 25 words",
  "reason":          "<= 40 words"
}
"""


# ================================================================
# LLM HELPER
# ================================================================
def call_llm(client: OpenAI, system_prompt: str,
             user_prompt: str) -> Optional[dict]:
    for attempt in range(MAX_RETRIES):
        try:
            resp = client.chat.completions.create(
                model=JUDGE_MODEL,
                temperature=TEMPERATURE,
                response_format={"type": "json_object"},
                messages=[
                    {"role": "system", "content": system_prompt},
                    {"role": "user",   "content": user_prompt},
                ],
            )
            return json.loads(resp.choices[0].message.content)
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"  [retry {attempt+1}/{MAX_RETRIES}] {e}")
            time.sleep(SLEEP_ON_FAIL)
    return None


def extract_theme(client, title: str, text: str, max_chars: int):
    user = (f"TITLE: {title}\n\n"
            f"===== STORY =====\n{truncate(text, max_chars)}\n")
    return call_llm(client, THEME_EXTRACT_PROMPT, user)


def compare_themes(client, theme_a: str, theme_b: str):
    user = (f"THEME_A (original):\n{theme_a}\n\n"
            f"THEME_B (generated):\n{theme_b}\n")
    return call_llm(client, THEME_COMPARE_PROMPT, user)


# ================================================================
# CACHE
# ================================================================
def load_cache() -> dict:
    if THEME_CACHE.exists():
        return json.loads(THEME_CACHE.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    return {}


def save_cache(cache: dict):
    OUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    THEME_CACHE.write_text(
        json.dumps(cache, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")


# ================================================================
# MAIN
# ================================================================
def main():
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    ap.add_argument("--limit", type=int, default=0,
                    help="Process only the first N originals (smoke test).")
    ap.add_argument("--refresh-cache", action="store_true",
                    help="Re-extract themes for all originals.")
    args = ap.parse_args()

    api_key = os.environ.get("OPENAI_API_KEY")
    if not api_key:
        raise SystemExit("Set OPENAI_API_KEY in your environment.")
    client = OpenAI(api_key=api_key)

    if not ORIGINAL_DIR.exists():
        raise SystemExit(f"Missing folder: {ORIGINAL_DIR}")

    # -------- discover originals --------
    originals = sorted(ORIGINAL_DIR.glob("*.txt"))
    indexed = [(story_id(p), p) for p in originals if story_id(p)]
    if args.limit:
        indexed = indexed[:args.limit]
    print(f"Found {len(indexed)} originals to evaluate:")
    for sid, p in indexed:
        print(f"  ID {sid:>4}  {p.name}")

    # -------- index model files --------
    model_index = {}
    for model, folder in MODEL_DIRS.items():
        if not folder.exists():
            print(f"[WARN] {folder} not found; skipping {model}")
            model_index[model] = {}
            continue
        m = {story_id(p): p for p in folder.glob("*.txt") if story_id(p)}
        model_index[model] = m
        print(f"  {model}: indexed {len(m)} files")

    OUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    # ============================================================
    # STAGE 1 - Extract theme of each ORIGINAL (cached)
    # ============================================================
    cache = {} if args.refresh_cache else load_cache()
    print(f"\nStage 1: Extracting themes from originals "
          f"({len(cache)} already cached)")
    for sid, p in tqdm(indexed, desc="Original themes"):
        key = f"{sid}::{p.name}"
        if key in cache and not args.refresh_cache:
            continue
        text = p.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
        result = extract_theme(client, p.stem, text, MAX_CHARS_ORIGINAL)
        if not result:
            print(f"  [FAIL] could not extract theme for {p.name}")
            continue
        cache[key] = result
        save_cache(cache)
    save_cache(cache)

    # ============================================================
    # STAGE 2 - For each generated story: extract its theme,
    #           then compare it to the original's theme.
    # ============================================================
    print("\nStage 2: Extracting & comparing generated-story themes")
    rows, failures = [], []
    for sid, orig_path in tqdm(indexed, desc="Judge"):
        key = f"{sid}::{orig_path.name}"
        orig_entry = cache.get(key)
        if not orig_entry:
            failures.append({"stage": "no_original_theme",
                             "story_id": sid,
                             "original": orig_path.name,
                             "model": "", "generated": ""})
            continue
        orig_theme = orig_entry.get("main_theme", "")
        title = orig_path.stem

        for model, idx in model_index.items():
            gen_path = idx.get(sid)
            if gen_path is None:
                failures.append({"stage": "pair_missing",
                                 "story_id": sid,
                                 "original": orig_path.name,
                                 "model": model, "generated": ""})
                continue

            gen_text = gen_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8",
                                          errors="replace")
            gen_entry = extract_theme(client, title, gen_text,
                                      MAX_CHARS_GENERATED)
            if not gen_entry:
                failures.append({"stage": "extract_gen",
                                 "story_id": sid,
                                 "original": orig_path.name,
                                 "model": model,
                                 "generated": gen_path.name})
                continue
            gen_theme = gen_entry.get("main_theme", "")

            cmp = compare_themes(client, orig_theme, gen_theme) or {}
            score = cmp.get("theme_overlap", 0.0)

            rows.append({
                "Story ID":          sid,
                "Model":             model,
                "Title":             title,
                "Original File":     orig_path.name,
                "Generated File":    gen_path.name,
                "Original Theme":    orig_theme,
                "Generated Theme":   gen_theme,
                "Theme Overlap":     score,
                "Theme Verdict":     cmp.get("verdict", ""),
                "Shared Essence":    cmp.get("shared_essence", ""),
                "Key Differences":   cmp.get("key_differences", ""),
                "Reason":            cmp.get("reason", ""),
                "Original Keywords":  " | ".join(
                    orig_entry.get("theme_keywords", [])),
                "Generated Keywords": " | ".join(
                    gen_entry.get("theme_keywords", [])),
            })

    if not rows:
        print("\n[ERROR] No successful judgments.")
        return

    df = pd.DataFrame(rows)
    df["Theme Overlap"] = pd.to_numeric(df["Theme Overlap"],
                                        errors="coerce").fillna(0.0)

    # Discrete bucket
    def bucket(x: float) -> str:
        if x >= T_SAME:    return "THEME_SAME"
        if x >= T_PARTIAL: return "THEME_PARTIAL"
        return "THEME_DIFFERENT"
    df["Theme Bucket"] = df["Theme Overlap"].apply(bucket)

    # ============================================================
    # AGGREGATES
    # ============================================================
    bucket_counts = (df.groupby(["Model", "Theme Bucket"]).size()
                       .unstack(fill_value=0)
                       .reindex(columns=["THEME_SAME", "THEME_PARTIAL",
                                         "THEME_DIFFERENT"],
                                fill_value=0))
    totals = bucket_counts.sum(axis=1).replace(0, 1)
    bucket_pct = bucket_counts.div(totals, axis=0).mul(100).round(2)
    bucket_counts["Total"] = bucket_counts.sum(axis=1)

    mean_overlap = (df.groupby("Model")["Theme Overlap"]
                      .agg(["mean", "median", "count"])
                      .round(3).reset_index())

    # ============================================================
    # WRITE EXCEL
    # ============================================================
    out_path = OUT_DIR / "theme_judgments.xlsx"
    with pd.ExcelWriter(out_path, engine="openpyxl") as w:
        df.to_excel(w, sheet_name="Raw", index=False)
        bucket_counts.to_excel(w, sheet_name="BucketCounts")
        bucket_pct.to_excel(w,    sheet_name="BucketPercent")
        mean_overlap.to_excel(w,  sheet_name="MeanThemeOverlap",
                              index=False)

        # Flat view of cached original themes
        orig_rows = []
        for k, v in cache.items():
            sid_, fname = k.split("::", 1)
            orig_rows.append({
                "Story ID":   sid_,
                "Original File": fname,
                "Main Theme": v.get("main_theme", ""),
                "Keywords":   " | ".join(v.get("theme_keywords", [])),
                "Evidence":   v.get("supporting_evidence", ""),
            })
        pd.DataFrame(orig_rows).to_excel(w, sheet_name="OriginalThemes",
                                         index=False)

        if failures:
            pd.DataFrame(failures).to_excel(w, sheet_name="Failures",
                                            index=False)

    # ============================================================
    # CONSOLE
    # ============================================================
    print("\n=== Theme-overlap bucket counts ===")
    print(bucket_counts.to_string())
    print("\n=== Theme-overlap bucket % per model ===")
    print(bucket_pct.to_string())
    print("\n=== Mean theme overlap per model ===")
    print(mean_overlap.to_string(index=False))
    if failures:
        print(f"\n[NOTE] {len(failures)} failures logged in 'Failures'.")
    print(f"\n[OK] Wrote {out_path}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

positioning_paragraph.tex.name: positioning_paragraph.tex
positioning_paragraph.tex.value:
The closest methodological neighbour of our work is the recent
large-scale network analysis of LLM-generated stories by~\cite{networkpaper2025},
which evaluates English outputs from GPT-4o and Gemini through signed
character-interaction graphs and finds a systematic positivity bias.
We differ on three dimensions. First, our setting is Urdu, a
low-resource Perso-Arabic-script language whose morphology, kinship
lexicon, and cultural scripts surface failure modes invisible in
English evaluation. Second, our evaluation is multi-axis rather than
purely structural: we audit linguistic correctness at the sentence
level (Section~\ref{subsec:linguistic_errors}), cultural fidelity at
the referent level (Section~\ref{sec:culture}), and creative vs
memorised plot reuse at the story level
(Section~\ref{sec:creativity}). Third, our model panel includes
DeepSeek and QWEN alongside GPT, allowing us to separate
English-trained from multilingual-pretrained behaviours. Within the
taxonomy of LLM story generation proposed by~\cite{teleki2025survey},
our work falls under independent generation with multi-axis automatic
evaluation, addressing the low-resource evaluation gap they identify
as under-explored. We additionally replicate~\cite{networkpaper2025}'s
character-network analysis on Urdu in Section~\ref{sec:networks},
confirming the positivity bias cross-lingually.

character_network_analysis.py.name: character_network_analysis.py
character_network_analysis.py.value:
"""
Character-network analysis of Urdu LLM-generated stories.
Replicates the methodology of arXiv:2510.18932 (signed character networks)
on a low-resource (Urdu) corpus, with a human-authored reference.

Pipeline
--------
For every story in:
    AIStories/stories/creativityTest/*.txt    (treated as HUMAN reference)
    AIStories/stories/GPT/*.txt
    AIStories/stories/DeepSeek/*.txt
    AIStories/stories/QWEN/*.txt

  1. Use GPT-4o-mini to extract a JSON list of characters and signed
     pairwise relationships (positive / negative / neutral) with a 1-3
     intensity weight.
  2. Build a signed undirected graph in NetworkX.
  3. Compute structural metrics:
        n_characters
        n_edges
        density
        signed_density
        avg_clustering
        positivity_ratio = pos_edges / (pos_edges + neg_edges)
        negativity_ratio = neg_edges / (pos_edges + neg_edges)
        avg_edge_weight
        top_character_degree_centrality
        protagonist_dominance     (top_degree / sum_of_degrees)
        n_components
  4. Aggregate per source (Human, GPT, DeepSeek, QWEN) and write Excel
     plus a JSON dump of every signed graph.

Outputs
-------
AIStories/network_results/
    network_extractions.json     (cached LLM outputs)
    network_metrics.xlsx
        Sheets:
          - PerStory
          - PerSource_Mean
          - PerSource_Median
          - Pairwise_HumanComparison

Usage
-----
    python character_network_analysis.py --limit 2     # smoke test
    python character_network_analysis.py               # full run
    python character_network_analysis.py --refresh-cache

Requires
--------
    pip install openai pandas openpyxl networkx tqdm
    export OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
"""

import argparse
import json
import os
import time
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Optional

import networkx as nx
import pandas as pd
from openai import OpenAI
from tqdm import tqdm

# ================================================================
# CONFIGURATION
# ================================================================
BASE = Path("AIStories/stories")
SOURCES = {
    "Human":    BASE / "creativityTest",   # the originals folder
    "GPT":      BASE / "GPT",
    "DeepSeek": BASE / "DeepSeek",
    "QWEN":     BASE / "QWEN",
}
OUT_DIR  = Path("AIStories/network_results")
CACHE_FP = OUT_DIR / "network_extractions.json"

JUDGE_MODEL   = "gpt-4o-mini"
TEMPERATURE   = 0.0
MAX_RETRIES   = 3
SLEEP_ON_FAIL = 4
MAX_CHARS     = 18000   # tolerate long originals; truncate longer

# ================================================================
# PROMPT
# ================================================================
EXTRACTION_PROMPT = """You are a literary analyst fluent in Urdu and
English. You will be given an Urdu story. Extract:

  1. The list of distinct CHARACTERS who actually appear or speak in
     the story. Skip purely mentioned-by-name characters with no role.
  2. The signed pairwise RELATIONSHIPS between those characters as
     evidenced by the story.

For each character, give:
  - name        (as it appears in the story, transliterated if needed)
  - role        (one of: "protagonist", "antagonist", "supporting",
                          "mentor", "love_interest", "antagonised_victim",
                          "narrator", "other")

For each relationship, give:
  - source         (character name)
  - target         (character name)
  - sign           ("positive", "negative", "neutral")
                   positive = affection, support, alliance, love, friendship
                   negative = conflict, hostility, betrayal, abuse, rivalry
                   neutral  = acquaintance / professional / functional
  - intensity     (1 = weak, 2 = moderate, 3 = strong)
  - evidence      (<= 20-word Urdu/English paraphrase from the story)

Rules:
  - Only include relationships that have textual support.
  - Use the SAME spelling of a name for both source and target across
    all edges of that character.
  - Do not invent characters not in the story.
  - Symmetric relationships should be listed ONCE (alphabetical
    source <= target if you can't tell who is "active").

Return STRICT JSON only:
{
  "characters": [
    {"name": "string", "role": "string"}
  ],
  "relationships": [
    {"source": "string", "target": "string",
     "sign": "positive|negative|neutral",
     "intensity": 1, "evidence": "string"}
  ]
}
Output JSON only, no other text.
"""


# ================================================================
# LLM HELPER
# ================================================================
def call_llm(client: OpenAI, system_prompt: str,
             user_prompt: str) -> Optional[dict]:
    for attempt in range(MAX_RETRIES):
        try:
            resp = client.chat.completions.create(
                model=JUDGE_MODEL,
                temperature=TEMPERATURE,
                response_format={"type": "json_object"},
                messages=[
                    {"role": "system", "content": system_prompt},
                    {"role": "user",   "content": user_prompt},
                ],
            )
            return json.loads(resp.choices[0].message.content)
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"  [retry {attempt+1}/{MAX_RETRIES}] {e}")
            time.sleep(SLEEP_ON_FAIL)
    return None


def truncate(text: str, max_chars: int = MAX_CHARS) -> str:
    return text if len(text) <= max_chars else text[:max_chars] + " […]"


def extract_network(client, title: str, text: str) -> Optional[dict]:
    user = (f"TITLE: {title}\n\n"
            f"===== STORY =====\n{truncate(text)}\n")
    return call_llm(client, EXTRACTION_PROMPT, user)


# ================================================================
# CACHE
# ================================================================
def load_cache() -> dict:
    if CACHE_FP.exists():
        return json.loads(CACHE_FP.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    return {}

def save_cache(cache: dict):
    OUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    CACHE_FP.write_text(
        json.dumps(cache, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")


# ================================================================
# GRAPH BUILDING
# ================================================================
SIGN_TO_NUM = {"positive": +1, "negative": -1, "neutral": 0}

def normalise_name(name: str) -> str:
    return (name or "").strip().lower()

def build_graph(extraction: dict) -> nx.Graph:
    """Undirected, signed, weighted graph."""
    G = nx.Graph()

    # add characters as nodes (use normalised key, keep display name as attr)
    for ch in extraction.get("characters", []):
        nm = normalise_name(ch.get("name", ""))
        if not nm:
            continue
        if nm not in G:
            G.add_node(nm,
                       display=ch.get("name", ""),
                       role=ch.get("role", "other"))

    # add edges
    for rel in extraction.get("relationships", []):
        u = normalise_name(rel.get("source", ""))
        v = normalise_name(rel.get("target", ""))
        if not u or not v or u == v:
            continue
        # if a node was not declared in characters list, add it
        for n in (u, v):
            if n not in G:
                G.add_node(n, display=n, role="other")
        sign = SIGN_TO_NUM.get(rel.get("sign", "neutral"), 0)
        intensity = int(rel.get("intensity", 1) or 1)
        weight = sign * intensity   # signed weight in [-3, +3]

        if G.has_edge(u, v):
            # merge: average weight, keep evidence as list
            old = G[u][v]
            old["weight_list"].append(weight)
            old["evidence_list"].append(rel.get("evidence", ""))
            old["weight"] = sum(old["weight_list"]) / len(old["weight_list"])
        else:
            G.add_edge(u, v,
                       weight=weight,
                       weight_list=[weight],
                       evidence_list=[rel.get("evidence", "")])
    return G


# ================================================================
# METRICS
# ================================================================
def compute_metrics(G: nx.Graph) -> dict:
    n = G.number_of_nodes()
    m = G.number_of_edges()

    pos_edges = sum(1 for _, _, d in G.edges(data=True) if d["weight"] >  0)
    neg_edges = sum(1 for _, _, d in G.edges(data=True) if d["weight"] <  0)
    neu_edges = sum(1 for _, _, d in G.edges(data=True) if d["weight"] == 0)

    signed = pos_edges + neg_edges
    pos_ratio = (pos_edges / signed) if signed else 0.0
    neg_ratio = (neg_edges / signed) if signed else 0.0
    neu_ratio = (neu_edges / m)      if m       else 0.0

    density = nx.density(G) if n > 1 else 0.0

    # signed density: average edge weight / max possible (3)
    avg_w = (sum(d["weight"] for _, _, d in G.edges(data=True)) / m
             if m else 0.0)
    signed_density = avg_w / 3.0

    # clustering on the unsigned graph
    try:
        avg_clustering = nx.average_clustering(G) if n >= 3 else 0.0
    except Exception:
        avg_clustering = 0.0

    # protagonist dominance (degree-centrality of top character)
    if n >= 2:
        cent = nx.degree_centrality(G)
        top_centrality = max(cent.values())
        deg_sum = sum(dict(G.degree()).values()) or 1
        top_deg  = max(dict(G.degree()).values())
        protagonist_dominance = top_deg / deg_sum
    else:
        top_centrality = 0.0
        protagonist_dominance = 0.0

    n_components = nx.number_connected_components(G) if n else 0

    return {
        "n_characters":           n,
        "n_edges":                m,
        "pos_edges":              pos_edges,
        "neg_edges":              neg_edges,
        "neu_edges":              neu_edges,
        "positivity_ratio":       round(pos_ratio, 3),
        "negativity_ratio":       round(neg_ratio, 3),
        "neutral_ratio":          round(neu_ratio, 3),
        "density":                round(density, 3),
        "signed_density":         round(signed_density, 3),
        "avg_edge_weight":        round(avg_w, 3),
        "avg_clustering":         round(avg_clustering, 3),
        "top_degree_centrality":  round(top_centrality, 3),
        "protagonist_dominance":  round(protagonist_dominance, 3),
        "n_components":           n_components,
    }


# ================================================================
# I/O HELPERS
# ================================================================
def graph_to_serialisable(G: nx.Graph) -> dict:
    return {
        "nodes": [
            {"id": n, "display": d.get("display", n),
             "role": d.get("role", "other")}
            for n, d in G.nodes(data=True)
        ],
        "edges": [
            {"source": u, "target": v,
             "weight": d.get("weight", 0),
             "evidence": " | ".join(d.get("evidence_list", []))}
            for u, v, d in G.edges(data=True)
        ],
    }


# ================================================================
# MAIN
# ================================================================
def main():
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    ap.add_argument("--limit", type=int, default=0,
                    help="Process only first N stories PER SOURCE.")
    ap.add_argument("--refresh-cache", action="store_true")
    args = ap.parse_args()

    api_key = os.environ.get("OPENAI_API_KEY")
    if not api_key:
        raise SystemExit("Set OPENAI_API_KEY in your environment.")
    client = OpenAI(api_key=api_key)

    # discover files
    files_by_source = {}
    for src, folder in SOURCES.items():
        if not folder.exists():
            print(f"[WARN] {folder} not found; skipping {src}")
            files_by_source[src] = []
            continue
        fs = sorted(folder.glob("*.txt"))
        if args.limit:
            fs = fs[:args.limit]
        files_by_source[src] = fs
        print(f"  {src:<9}: {len(fs)} stories")

    OUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    cache = {} if args.refresh_cache else load_cache()
    per_story_rows = []
    graphs_dump = {}

    # ============================================================
    # EXTRACT + COMPUTE
    # ============================================================
    for src, files in files_by_source.items():
        for fp in tqdm(files, desc=f"{src}"):
            key = f"{src}::{fp.name}"

            if key not in cache or args.refresh_cache:
                text = fp.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
                ext = extract_network(client, fp.stem, text)
                if not ext:
                    print(f"  [FAIL] {fp.name}")
                    continue
                cache[key] = ext
                save_cache(cache)        # incremental save

            ext = cache[key]
            G = build_graph(ext)
            metrics = compute_metrics(G)

            row = {
                "Source":   src,
                "Story":    fp.name,
                "Title":    fp.stem,
                **metrics,
            }
            per_story_rows.append(row)

            graphs_dump[key] = {
                "metrics":   metrics,
                "graph":     graph_to_serialisable(G),
            }

    save_cache(cache)

    if not per_story_rows:
        print("\n[ERROR] No stories processed.")
        return

    df = pd.DataFrame(per_story_rows)

    # ============================================================
    # AGGREGATES per source
    # ============================================================
    metric_cols = [
        "n_characters", "n_edges",
        "density", "signed_density",
        "avg_edge_weight", "avg_clustering",
        "positivity_ratio", "negativity_ratio", "neutral_ratio",
        "top_degree_centrality", "protagonist_dominance",
        "n_components",
    ]
    per_source_mean   = (df.groupby("Source")[metric_cols]
                           .mean().round(3))
    per_source_median = (df.groupby("Source")[metric_cols]
                           .median().round(3))

    # ============================================================
    # HUMAN-BASELINE COMPARISON
    # ============================================================
    pairwise_rows = []
    if "Human" in per_source_mean.index:
        human_mean = per_source_mean.loc["Human"]
        for src in per_source_mean.index:
            if src == "Human":
                continue
            for col in metric_cols:
                h = human_mean[col]
                m = per_source_mean.loc[src, col]
                pairwise_rows.append({
                    "Model":   src,
                    "Metric":  col,
                    "Human":   h,
                    "Model_Mean": m,
                    "Delta (Model - Human)": round(m - h, 3),
                    "Relative (Model/Human)":
                        round(m / h, 3) if h else None,
                })
    pairwise_df = pd.DataFrame(pairwise_rows)

    # ============================================================
    # WRITE EXCEL
    # ============================================================
    out_path = OUT_DIR / "network_metrics.xlsx"
    with pd.ExcelWriter(out_path, engine="openpyxl") as w:
        df.to_excel(w, sheet_name="PerStory", index=False)
        per_source_mean.to_excel(w,   sheet_name="PerSource_Mean")
        per_source_median.to_excel(w, sheet_name="PerSource_Median")
        if not pairwise_df.empty:
            pairwise_df.to_excel(w,
                sheet_name="Pairwise_HumanComparison", index=False)

    # dump signed graphs JSON for re-plotting / sharing
    (OUT_DIR / "signed_graphs.json").write_text(
        json.dumps(graphs_dump, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2),
        encoding="utf-8")

    # ============================================================
    # CONSOLE
    # ============================================================
    print("\n=== Mean metrics per source ===")
    print(per_source_mean.to_string())
    print("\n=== Median metrics per source ===")
    print(per_source_median.to_string())
    if not pairwise_df.empty:
        print("\n=== Vs Human reference (deltas) ===")
        pivot = (pairwise_df
                 .pivot(index="Metric", columns="Model",
                        values="Delta (Model - Human)")
                 .round(3))
        print(pivot.to_string())
    print(f"\n[OK] Wrote {out_path}")
    print(f"[OK] Wrote signed_graphs.json")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

"""
Character-network analysis of Urdu LLM-generated stories.
Replicates the methodology of arXiv:2510.18932 (signed character networks)
on a low-resource (Urdu) corpus, with a human-authored reference.

Pipeline
--------
For every story in:
    AIStories/stories/creativityTest/*.txt    (treated as HUMAN reference)
    AIStories/stories/GPT/*.txt
    AIStories/stories/DeepSeek/*.txt
    AIStories/stories/QWEN/*.txt

  1. Use GPT-4o-mini to extract a JSON list of characters and signed
     pairwise relationships (positive / negative / neutral) with a 1-3
     intensity weight.
  2. Build a signed undirected graph in NetworkX.
  3. Compute structural metrics:
        n_characters
        n_edges
        density
        mean_signed_weight    (in [-1, +1])
        avg_clustering
        positivity_ratio      (pos_edges / signed_edges)
        negativity_ratio      (neg_edges / signed_edges)
        neutral_ratio         (neu_edges / total_edges)
        avg_edge_weight
        protagonist_share     (top_degree / sum_of_degrees)
        top_eigen_centrality  (max eigenvector centrality)
        n_components
        has_signed_edges      (boolean - useful for filtering)
  4. Aggregate per source (Human, GPT, DeepSeek, QWEN) and write Excel
     plus a JSON dump of every signed graph.

Outputs
-------
AIStories/network_results/
    network_extractions.json
    signed_graphs.json
    network_metrics.xlsx
        Sheets:
          - PerStory
          - PerSource_Mean
          - PerSource_Median
          - PerSource_SignedOnly      (positivity ratio only over stories
                                       that have at least one signed edge)
          - Pairwise_HumanComparison

Usage
-----
    python character_network_analysis.py --limit 2     # smoke test
    python character_network_analysis.py               # full run
    python character_network_analysis.py --refresh-cache

Requires
--------
    pip install openai pandas openpyxl networkx numpy scipy tqdm
    export OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
"""

import argparse
import json
import os
import time
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Optional

import networkx as nx
import pandas as pd
from openai import OpenAI
from tqdm import tqdm

# ================================================================
# CONFIGURATION
# ================================================================
BASE = Path("AIStories/stories")
SOURCES = {
    "Human":    BASE / "creativityTest",
    "GPT":      BASE / "GPT",
    "DeepSeek": BASE / "DeepSeek",
    "QWEN":     BASE / "QWEN",
}
OUT_DIR  = Path("AIStories/network_results")
CACHE_FP = OUT_DIR / "network_extractions.json"

JUDGE_MODEL   = "gpt-4o-mini"
TEMPERATURE   = 0.0
MAX_RETRIES   = 3
SLEEP_ON_FAIL = 4
MAX_CHARS     = 18000

SIGN_TO_NUM = {"positive": +1, "negative": -1, "neutral": 0}

METRIC_COLS = [
    "n_characters", "n_edges",
    "density", "mean_signed_weight",
    "avg_edge_weight", "avg_clustering",
    "positivity_ratio", "negativity_ratio", "neutral_ratio",
    "protagonist_share", "top_eigen_centrality",
    "n_components",
]

# ================================================================
# PROMPT
# ================================================================
EXTRACTION_PROMPT = """You are a literary analyst fluent in Urdu and
English. You will be given an Urdu story. Extract:

  1. The list of distinct CHARACTERS who actually appear or speak in
     the story. Skip purely mentioned-by-name characters with no role.
  2. The signed pairwise RELATIONSHIPS between those characters as
     evidenced by the story.

For each character, give:
  - name        (as it appears in the story, transliterated if needed)
  - role        (one of: "protagonist", "antagonist", "supporting",
                          "mentor", "love_interest", "antagonised_victim",
                          "narrator", "other")

For each relationship, give:
  - source         (character name)
  - target         (character name)
  - sign           ("positive", "negative", "neutral")
                   positive = affection, support, alliance, love, friendship
                   negative = conflict, hostility, betrayal, abuse, rivalry
                   neutral  = acquaintance / professional / functional
  - intensity     (1 = weak, 2 = moderate, 3 = strong)
  - evidence      (<= 20-word Urdu/English paraphrase from the story)

Rules:
  - Only include relationships that have textual support.
  - Use the SAME spelling of a name for both source and target across
    all edges of that character.
  - Do not invent characters not in the story.
  - Symmetric relationships should be listed ONCE.

Return STRICT JSON only:
{
  "characters": [
    {"name": "string", "role": "string"}
  ],
  "relationships": [
    {"source": "string", "target": "string",
     "sign": "positive|negative|neutral",
     "intensity": 1, "evidence": "string"}
  ]
}
Output JSON only, no other text.
"""


# ================================================================
# LLM HELPER
# ================================================================
def call_llm(client: OpenAI, system_prompt: str,
             user_prompt: str) -> Optional[dict]:
    for attempt in range(MAX_RETRIES):
        try:
            resp = client.chat.completions.create(
                model=JUDGE_MODEL,
                temperature=TEMPERATURE,
                response_format={"type": "json_object"},
                messages=[
                    {"role": "system", "content": system_prompt},
                    {"role": "user",   "content": user_prompt},
                ],
            )
            return json.loads(resp.choices[0].message.content)
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"  [retry {attempt+1}/{MAX_RETRIES}] {e}")
            time.sleep(SLEEP_ON_FAIL)
    return None


def truncate(text: str, max_chars: int = MAX_CHARS) -> str:
    return text if len(text) <= max_chars else text[:max_chars] + " […]"


def extract_network(client, title: str, text: str) -> Optional[dict]:
    user = (f"TITLE: {title}\n\n"
            f"===== STORY =====\n{truncate(text)}\n")
    return call_llm(client, EXTRACTION_PROMPT, user)


# ================================================================
# CACHE
# ================================================================
def load_cache() -> dict:
    if CACHE_FP.exists():
        try:
            return json.loads(CACHE_FP.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
        except json.JSONDecodeError:
            print("[WARN] cache file corrupt; starting fresh")
    return {}

def save_cache(cache: dict):
    OUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    CACHE_FP.write_text(
        json.dumps(cache, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")


# ================================================================
# GRAPH BUILDING
# ================================================================
def normalise_name(name: str) -> str:
    return (name or "").strip().lower()

def build_graph(extraction: dict) -> nx.Graph:
    """Undirected, signed, weighted graph."""
    G = nx.Graph()

    for ch in extraction.get("characters", []) or []:
        nm = normalise_name(ch.get("name", ""))
        if not nm:
            continue
        if nm not in G:
            G.add_node(nm,
                       display=ch.get("name", ""),
                       role=ch.get("role", "other"))

    for rel in extraction.get("relationships", []) or []:
        u = normalise_name(rel.get("source", ""))
        v = normalise_name(rel.get("target", ""))
        if not u or not v or u == v:
            continue
        for n in (u, v):
            if n not in G:
                G.add_node(n, display=n, role="other")

        sign = SIGN_TO_NUM.get((rel.get("sign") or "neutral").lower(), 0)
        try:
            intensity = int(rel.get("intensity", 1) or 1)
        except (TypeError, ValueError):
            intensity = 1
        intensity = max(1, min(intensity, 3))
        weight = sign * intensity

        if G.has_edge(u, v):
            old = G[u][v]
            old["weight_list"].append(weight)
            old["evidence_list"].append(rel.get("evidence", ""))
            old["weight"] = sum(old["weight_list"]) / len(old["weight_list"])
        else:
            G.add_edge(u, v,
                       weight=weight,
                       weight_list=[weight],
                       evidence_list=[rel.get("evidence", "")])
    return G


# ================================================================
# METRICS
# ================================================================
def compute_metrics(G: nx.Graph) -> dict:
    n = G.number_of_nodes()
    m = G.number_of_edges()

    pos_edges = sum(1 for _, _, d in G.edges(data=True) if d["weight"] >  0)
    neg_edges = sum(1 for _, _, d in G.edges(data=True) if d["weight"] <  0)
    neu_edges = sum(1 for _, _, d in G.edges(data=True) if d["weight"] == 0)
    signed    = pos_edges + neg_edges

    pos_ratio = (pos_edges / signed) if signed else 0.0
    neg_ratio = (neg_edges / signed) if signed else 0.0
    neu_ratio = (neu_edges / m)      if m       else 0.0

    density = nx.density(G) if n > 1 else 0.0

    avg_w = ((sum(d["weight"] for _, _, d in G.edges(data=True)) / m)
             if m else 0.0)
    mean_signed_weight = avg_w / 3.0   # scaled to [-1, +1]

    try:
        avg_clustering = nx.average_clustering(G) if n >= 3 else 0.0
    except Exception:
        avg_clustering = 0.0

    # ----- protagonist_share (replaces noisy top_degree_centrality) -----
    if n >= 2 and m >= 1:
        degs    = dict(G.degree())
        deg_sum = sum(degs.values()) or 1
        top_deg = max(degs.values())
        protagonist_share = top_deg / deg_sum
    else:
        protagonist_share = 0.0

    # ----- top eigenvector centrality (robust call) -----
    top_eigen = 0.0
    if n >= 3 and m >= 2:
        # eigenvector centrality is only well-defined on the largest
        # connected component with at least one edge; isolate it.
        try:
            components = list(nx.connected_components(G))
            largest = max(components, key=len)
            H = G.subgraph(largest).copy()
            if H.number_of_nodes() >= 3 and H.number_of_edges() >= 2:
                try:
                    eig = nx.eigenvector_centrality_numpy(H, weight=None)
                except Exception:
                    eig = nx.eigenvector_centrality(
                        H, max_iter=1000, tol=1e-04, weight=None)
                if eig:
                    top_eigen = max(eig.values())
        except Exception as e:
            # absolutely no failure should propagate from a metric calc
            print(f"  [WARN] eigenvector centrality skipped: {e}")
            top_eigen = 0.0

    n_components = nx.number_connected_components(G) if n else 0

    return {
        "n_characters":         n,
        "n_edges":              m,
        "pos_edges":            pos_edges,
        "neg_edges":            neg_edges,
        "neu_edges":            neu_edges,
        "positivity_ratio":     round(pos_ratio, 3),
        "negativity_ratio":     round(neg_ratio, 3),
        "neutral_ratio":        round(neu_ratio, 3),
        "density":              round(density, 3),
        "mean_signed_weight":   round(mean_signed_weight, 3),
        "avg_edge_weight":      round(avg_w, 3),
        "avg_clustering":       round(avg_clustering, 3),
        "protagonist_share":    round(protagonist_share, 3),
        "top_eigen_centrality": round(top_eigen, 3),
        "n_components":         n_components,
        "has_signed_edges":     signed > 0,
    }


# ================================================================
# I/O HELPERS
# ================================================================
def graph_to_serialisable(G: nx.Graph) -> dict:
    return {
        "nodes": [
            {"id": n, "display": d.get("display", n),
             "role": d.get("role", "other")}
            for n, d in G.nodes(data=True)
        ],
        "edges": [
            {"source": u, "target": v,
             "weight": d.get("weight", 0),
             "evidence": " | ".join(d.get("evidence_list", []))}
            for u, v, d in G.edges(data=True)
        ],
    }


# ================================================================
# MAIN
# ================================================================
def main():
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    ap.add_argument("--limit", type=int, default=0,
                    help="Process only first N stories PER SOURCE.")
    ap.add_argument("--refresh-cache", action="store_true")
    args = ap.parse_args()

    api_key = os.environ.get("OPENAI_API_KEY")
    if not api_key:
        raise SystemExit("Set OPENAI_API_KEY in your environment.")
    client = OpenAI(api_key=api_key)

    # ---- discover files ----
    files_by_source = {}
    for src, folder in SOURCES.items():
        if not folder.exists():
            print(f"[WARN] {folder} not found; skipping {src}")
            files_by_source[src] = []
            continue
        fs = sorted(folder.glob("*.txt"))
        if args.limit:
            fs = fs[:args.limit]
        files_by_source[src] = fs
        print(f"  {src:<9}: {len(fs)} stories")

    OUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    cache = {} if args.refresh_cache else load_cache()
    per_story_rows = []
    graphs_dump    = {}

    # ============================================================
    # EXTRACT + COMPUTE
    # ============================================================
    for src, files in files_by_source.items():
        for fp in tqdm(files, desc=f"{src}"):
            key = f"{src}::{fp.name}"

            if key not in cache or args.refresh_cache:
                text = fp.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
                ext = extract_network(client, fp.stem, text)
                if not ext:
                    print(f"  [FAIL] {fp.name}")
                    continue
                cache[key] = ext
                save_cache(cache)

            ext = cache[key]
            try:
                G = build_graph(ext)
                metrics = compute_metrics(G)
            except Exception as e:
                print(f"  [ERR] {fp.name}: {e}")
                continue

            per_story_rows.append({
                "Source": src,
                "Story":  fp.name,
                "Title":  fp.stem,
                **metrics,
            })
            graphs_dump[key] = {
                "metrics": metrics,
                "graph":   graph_to_serialisable(G),
            }

    save_cache(cache)

    if not per_story_rows:
        print("\n[ERROR] No stories processed.")
        return

    df = pd.DataFrame(per_story_rows)

    # ============================================================
    # AGGREGATES per source
    # ============================================================
    per_source_mean   = (df.groupby("Source")[METRIC_COLS]
                           .mean().round(3))
    per_source_median = (df.groupby("Source")[METRIC_COLS]
                           .median().round(3))

    # Positivity / negativity only over stories with >=1 signed edge.
    mask = df["has_signed_edges"]
    signed_only = (df[mask].groupby("Source")
                            [["positivity_ratio", "negativity_ratio",
                              "mean_signed_weight"]]
                            .mean().round(3))
    # how many stories per source actually had a signed edge
    signed_counts = (df.groupby("Source")["has_signed_edges"]
                       .agg(["sum", "count"])
                       .rename(columns={"sum": "stories_with_signed_edges",
                                        "count": "total_stories"}))
    signed_only = signed_only.join(signed_counts, how="outer")

    # ============================================================
    # HUMAN-BASELINE COMPARISON
    # ============================================================
    pairwise_rows = []
    if "Human" in per_source_mean.index:
        human_mean = per_source_mean.loc["Human"]
        for src in per_source_mean.index:
            if src == "Human":
                continue
            for col in METRIC_COLS:
                h = human_mean[col]
                m = per_source_mean.loc[src, col]
                rel = (round(m / h, 3) if (h not in (0, None)
                                           and pd.notna(h)) else None)
                pairwise_rows.append({
                    "Model":   src,
                    "Metric":  col,
                    "Human":   h,
                    "Model_Mean": m,
                    "Delta (Model - Human)": round(m - h, 3),
                    "Relative (Model/Human)": rel,
                })
    pairwise_df = pd.DataFrame(pairwise_rows)

    # ============================================================
    # WRITE EXCEL
    # ============================================================
    out_path = OUT_DIR / "network_metrics.xlsx"
    with pd.ExcelWriter(out_path, engine="openpyxl") as w:
        df.to_excel(w, sheet_name="PerStory", index=False)
        per_source_mean.to_excel(w,   sheet_name="PerSource_Mean")
        per_source_median.to_excel(w, sheet_name="PerSource_Median")
        signed_only.to_excel(w,       sheet_name="PerSource_SignedOnly")
        if not pairwise_df.empty:
            pairwise_df.to_excel(w,
                sheet_name="Pairwise_HumanComparison", index=False)

    (OUT_DIR / "signed_graphs.json").write_text(
        json.dumps(graphs_dump, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2),
        encoding="utf-8")

    # ============================================================
    # CONSOLE
    # ============================================================
    print("\n=== Mean metrics per source ===")
    print(per_source_mean.to_string())
    print("\n=== Median metrics per source ===")
    print(per_source_median.to_string())
    print("\n=== Positivity / negativity (signed-edge stories only) ===")
    print(signed_only.to_string())
    if not pairwise_df.empty:
        print("\n=== Vs Human reference (deltas) ===")
        pivot = (pairwise_df
                 .pivot(index="Metric", columns="Model",
                        values="Delta (Model - Human)")
                 .round(3))
        print(pivot.to_string())
    print(f"\n[OK] Wrote {out_path}")
    print(f"[OK] Wrote {OUT_DIR/'signed_graphs.json'}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
